evidence_rows.reading: A pause is only justified if the checked hunt has already covered the intended high-priority carry tracks.
If every checked high-priority symbol closes inactive, blindly moving to lower-priority tracks risks replay drift rather than real carry discovery.
The main V1.2 bottleneck can remain carry row diversity even while a specific v4 hunt slice is temporarily exhausted.

interpretation: The current v4 q2/A hunt has now checked the intended high-priority basis-spread and carry-duration targets without surfacing an active structural lane.
That does not solve the main V1.2 bottleneck, but it does justify a pause before touching lower-priority tracks such as cross-dataset reuse or exit-alignment.
So the correct next move is a short reassessment of the v4 hunt posture rather than another immediate symbol replay.
